commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=994c50290440d7d526f51c2f7916349a7d262380)
commands.review_summary:
The PR imports existing usage helpers into `followup-runner` and appends a usage footer to queued followup delivery payloads when `responseUsage` is enabled.

PR surface: Source +39. Total +39 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main persists followup usage and sends final channel payloads without reading `responseUsage` or appending the usage footer. I did not run a live Telegram turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers and the contributor need to repair full-footer parity, provide live Telegram proof, and choose the canonical same-regression fix path; automation should not queue a repair while those remain human-owned.

target.title: fix(followup): render usage footer in followup-runner delivery for Telegram channels
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: The `/usage` command correctly sets `responseUsage` on the session entry, but the followup-runner delivery path (used by Telegram and most non-webchat channels) never rendered the usage footer. Only agent-runner (webchat) had this feature, creating an inconsistency where `/usage on` had no visible effect on Telegram.

**Solution**: Add response-usage footer rendering to the followup-runner delivery path, matching the existing agent-runner pattern. Guards against preserved-handoff/internal replies so usage footers don't leak into inter-session messages like `subagent_announce`.

**What changed**: `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts`: +39 — imports existing usage helpers and appends usage footer to delivery payloads when `responseUsage` is enabled. Three new imports: `hasNonzeroUsage`, `resolveModelCostConfig`, `resolveResponseUsageMode`, `appendUsageLine`, `formatResponseUsageLine`.

**What did NOT change**: Agent-runner webchat usage footer — untouched. `/usage` command behavior — untouched. The existing usage infrastructure — only imported, not modified.

Fixes #93905

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Telegram and other non-webchat channels never showed usage footers even when `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` was active. The followup-runner delivery path simply didn't check `responseUsage`.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-93905-telegram-usage-footer` rebased onto `upstream/main`
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` (project-standard wrapper)
- **Vitest**: 4.1.8

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` at Node 24.13 Linux x86_64

**After-fix evidence**:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.src.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  83 passed (83)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 28.79s
```

All 83 followup-runner tests pass including existing usage-related tests.

**Observed result after the fix**: When `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` is set, the followup-runner now reads `responseUsage` from the session store, checks `hasNonzeroUsage`, resolves the cost config, formats the usage line via `formatResponseUsageLine`, and appends it to delivery payloads via `appendUsageLine`. Three safeguards prevent leakage: `responseUsageMode !== "off"` check, `hasNonzeroUsage` guard, and `!preserveUserFacingSessionState` (suppresses usage on preserved-handoff/internal replies like `subagent_announce`). For total-only usage where `formatResponseUsageLine` returns null, a minimal `Usage · session \`key\`` line is appended so `/usage full` still provides context.

**What was not tested**: Live Telegram bot with `/usage on` and real model turn producing measurable token usage. This requires a configured Telegram bot token and a model that produces non-zero usage. The fix reuses existing usage formatting infrastructure that is already tested in agent-runner context; the only new code is the integration in the followup delivery path.

**Evidence provenance**: Terminal output from real local checkout of rebased branch on Node 24.13.1 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-23.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ 83/83 | followup-runner.test.ts — all passing |
| Rebase | ✅ | Clean rebase onto upstream/main, 1 commit, 1 file |

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?**

The `!preserveUserFacingSessionState` guard must correctly identify internal/handoff messages to prevent usage footers from leaking into `subagent_announce` and similar inter-session messages. If the guard is too loose, usage footers could appear in unexpected contexts; if too strict, legitimate followup deliveries might never show usage.

**Risk level**: Low risk — the guard directly follows the agent-runner pattern: `!preserveUserFacingSessionState` is already used by agent-runner for the same purpose. The `hasNonzeroUsage` guard prevents empty footers. The total-only fallback ensures `/usage full` mode always has some output.

**Mitigation**: The `!preserveUserFacingSessionState` flag is set by `shouldPreserveUserFacingSessionStateForInputProvenance` which identifies internal/admin sessions where the user-facing state should not be modified. The existing 83 tests exercise the full followup delivery pipeline.

**Merge risk declaration**:
- `merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery` — Appends additional content to delivery payloads for Telegram and other followup channels. The usage line is appended to existing payloads and does not replace any content.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. Code is mature (review feedback incorporated, both review findings addressed). The `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` gate requires maintainer decision on proof adequacy.
target.author: zhangqueping

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
